title: DataOps
text:
Objective

Transform data into a strategic banking asset

Eliminate data silos and enable integrated decision-making

Build a scalable, governed, and AI-ready organization

title: DataOps
text:
The Core Problem: Data Silos

Fragmented systems across:

Core banking

CRM

Risk

Marketing

Other Sources

Consequences:

Inconsistent KPIs

Slow decision-making

Poor customer experience

Limited AI capability
Missing Single Source of Truth

title: DataOps
text: Data Silos in Banking

title: DataOps
text: Data Silo management

title: DataOps
text:
Why Silos Persist

Organizational structure (departmental ownership)

Lack of governance and ownership

Technology fragmentation

Cultural resistance to sharing data

title: DataOps
text:
Cultural

Data exists, but is not trusted or shared

\[\downarrow\]




Experience-driven

Not data-driven



\[\downarrow\]



Teams optimize locally, not globally

title: DataOps
text:
The Role of the CDO
Bridge:

Business

Technology

Governance


Shift organization from: Siloed thinking \(\rightarrow\) Integrated, data-driven culture

title: DataOps
text: Change Agent Model

title: DataOps
text:
From Silos to Integrated Bank






flowchart LR
    A[Siloed Systems] --> B[Integrated Data Platform]
    B --> C[Trusted Data]
    C --> D[Analytics & AI]
    D --> E[Business Value]

title: DataOps
text:
Data Strategy Model

Combine:

Execution Steps (6-step framework)

Foundational Pillars (4 pillars)


Goal: > Move from strategy → execution → culture adoption

title: DataOps
text:
Integrated Framework





flowchart TB

    subgraph Steps
        A[Listen]
        B[Assess]
        C[Apply]
        D[Test]
        E[Execute]
        F[Scale]
    end

    subgraph Pillars
        P1[Data]
        P2[Technology]
        P3[Workflow]
        P4[Culture]
    end

    A --> B --> C --> D --> E --> F

    P1 --- A
    P2 --- C
    P3 --- E
    P4 --- F

title: DataOps
text:
Listen Data

Align with business objectives:

Revenue

Risk

Efficiency

Identify critical data domains:

Customer

Transactions

Risk

title: DataOps
text:
Assess + Technology

Evaluate:

Current architecture

Data pipelines

Integration gaps

Identify:

Legacy constraints

Scalability limitations

title: DataOps
text:
Apply + Workflow

Embed data into:

Credit decisioning

Customer journeys

Marketing campaigns


Move from: Reporting \(\rightarrow\) Operational intelligence

title: DataOps
text:
Test + Governance

Ensure:

Data quality

Security

Compliance

Define:

Ownership

Policies

Controls

title: DataOps
text:
Execute and Value Delivery

Build MVPs (small gains):

Churn prediction

Campaign targeting

Risk scoring
Automations


Deliver:

Fast, measurable impact

title: DataOps
text:
Scale + Culture

Expand successful use cases

Enable:

Data literacy

Self-service analytics


Create:

Organization-wide adoption

title: DataOps
text:
The Four Pillars (Foundation)

Data: \(\rightarrow\) Trusted and high-quality

Technology: \(\rightarrow\) Scalable and integrated

Workflow: \(\rightarrow\) Embedded into operations

Culture: \(\rightarrow\) Data-driven mindset

title: DataOps
text:
Cultural Transformation Journey





flowchart LR
    A[Data Awareness] --> B[Trust in Data]
    B --> C[Adoption]
    C --> D[Data-Driven Decisions]

title: DataOps
text:
Success Requires Alignment

Data, Technology, Workflow, Culture must move in lockstep
Failure occurs when:

Tech advances faster than culture

Data exists without governance

title: DataOps
text:
Target Architecture (Simplified)





flowchart TB
    A[Data Sources] --> B[Data Platform]
    B --> C[Governance Layer]
    C --> D[Analytics & AI]
    D --> E[Business Decisions]

title: DataOps
text:
Key Success Factors

Executive alignment
Strong governance
Use-case driven execution
Continuous iteration

title: DataOps
text:
Final Message

The biggest barrier is not data or technology — it is organizational culture


Break silos
Build trust
Deliver value
Scale adoption

title: DataOps
text:
Closing

Data strategy must:

Start with business

Deliver value quickly

Scale through culture

The CDO role: > Lead transformation, not just data
